candidates.stmt: Ahmed has lived in Beacon & Bents for 25 years and since 2016 has worked in it too. He served as an Independent councillor for the ward from 2008 to 2012 and remains active in the community, using his experience to address local issues and help local people. Known for speaking directly on important matters, Ahmed is committed to representing local interests. His key goals include banning future HMOs, enforcing zero tolerance for anti-social behaviour, improving adult social care and children's services, ending excessive salaries, scrapping town centre parking charges, revitalising the town centre, promoting tourism, and increasing council spending with local businesses.

I am standing in the ward of Boldon Colliery in the 7th May 2026 local elections. Unfortunately as it stands the Liberal Democrats are not well represented within South Tyneside and I believe that the values of the Liberal Democrats are essential to the future of the area, offering hope & unity over fear & division.

The Liberal Democrats exist to build and safeguard a fair, free and open society, in which we seek to balance the fundamental values of liberty, equality and community, and in which no-one shall be enslaved by poverty, ignorance or conformity.

Printed (hosted) by Democracy Club. Published and Promoted by Dr Phil Barron on behalf of Thomas William Stradling, Liberal Democrats, both at 3 Baldwin Avenue, East Boldon NE36 0EL

I am standing in the Cleadon Village and East Boldon ward for the elections on Thursday 7 May 2026. The Liberal Democrats have significant representation in various parts of Tyne and Wear and in County Durham, but for some reason not in South Tyneside. It is vital that this lack of representation is addressed.

The Liberal Democrats exist to build and safeguard a fair, free and open society, in which we seek to balance the fundamental values of liberty, equality and community and in which no-one shall be enslaved by poverty, ignorance or conformity.

Local government has suffered from many years of underfunding, resulting in weakened services, which struggle to meet needs. We need to fight for greater local autonomy and devolution. We need to ensure that new housing developments, which are desperately needed, are planned carefully with adequate infrastructure, that South Tyneside's contribution to the transition to renewable energy is made while avoiding harm to the environment, that the crises in health and social care are resolved on a cross-party and no-party consensus and that our local living environments are safe, secure and areas which we can be proud of. I encourage local people to get engaged and do their bit.

Printed (Hosted) by Democracy Club, Published and Promoted by Dr Phil Barron, Liberal Democrats, 3 Baldwin Avenue, East Boldon NE36 0EL

I was asked to stand as a candidate for Fellgate and Hedworth so here I am. 
I am patriotic but that's because I love our people.
Because I love our people in all their diversity I want to serve them and improve the quality of life for all of us.
I believe hate is our only enemy.
Hate can never build or create community cohesion.
I believe love is true life and our own personal sense of right and wrong is our Humanity.
My only promise is that I will do my best to serve and listen to anyone who needs help with problems relating to the Council. I don't have an ego, I'm not particularly clever but my heart is true. xx

"It is an honour to have been selected to be the candidate for my home ward of Westoe, in the 2026 local elections. I was born and raised in Westoe, and it’s where my heart will always be. After studying to become a teaching assistant at South Tyneside College, I spent over a decade working in education, specialising in special needs. I understand the importance of strong, inclusive education, and I will always fight for better funding to ensure every child has the support they deserve. Recently I made a career change and now help people with their housing and neighbourhood needs, making a difference in their community every day.”

“As a younger candidate, I bring a fresh perspective and energy to the role of councillor. I’m committed to giving Westoe’s youth a real voice and creating opportunities for them to thrive. Westoe deserves more. For too long, some areas have been overlooked, with more affluent parts receiving priority for things like parks and barriers, while other communities have fallen behind. I believe in governing with care, putting people first, and ensuring all areas of Westoe receive equal attention and resources. Together, we can build a future where everyone has the chance to succeed”
